title: Aggressive Equity Suitability Standards
content:
Meridian Wealth Partners maintains stringent suitability standards for aggressive equity investments to ensure full compliance with FINRA Rule 2111(a) reasonable-basis suitability requirements and customer-specific suitability obligations under FINRA Rule 2090. This policy governs the recommendation and placement of high-risk equity securities, sector-concentrated funds, leveraged equity products, emerging market equities, small-cap growth securities, and equity derivatives.

Client Qualification Criteria: Advisors must verify that clients meet minimum financial thresholds before recommending aggressive equity strategies. Clients must maintain liquid net worth exceeding $500,000, excluding primary residence, with annual income of at least $150,000 or total investable assets above $1.2 million. The proposed aggressive equity allocation cannot exceed 25% of the client's total portfolio value, or 35% for accredited investors with liquid net worth above $2 million.

Risk Tolerance Assessment: Per FINRA Rule 2090 know-your-customer obligations, advisors must document client risk tolerance through our standardized assessment tool. Clients must score 7 or higher on our 10-point risk scale and acknowledge understanding that aggressive equity investments may experience volatility exceeding 30% annually. Investment time horizon must be minimum seven years, with clients confirming they will not require access to invested principal for at least five years.

Product-Specific Guidelines: Individual equity positions in companies with market capitalizations below $2 billion require additional documentation of sector expertise and fundamental analysis. Leveraged equity ETFs carrying expense ratios above 0.75% or daily reset mechanisms require separate risk disclosure and cannot exceed 5% of aggressive equity allocation. Emerging market equity exposure is limited to 40% of the aggressive equity sleeve, with single-country funds restricted to 15% maximum allocation.

Documentation Requirements: All aggressive equity recommendations must include written investment thesis, expected holding period, exit strategy, and correlation analysis with existing portfolio holdings. Advisors must complete quarterly suitability reviews documenting performance attribution, risk-adjusted returns, and any material changes in client circumstances. FINRA Rule 2111(a) compliance requires maintaining comprehensive records of reasonable-basis due diligence, including third-party research, expense analysis, and performance benchmarking against appropriate indices.

Rebalancing and Monitoring: Positions exceeding 8% individual security weighting trigger mandatory rebalancing within 30 days. Sector concentration above 25% in any GICS sector classification requires portfolio committee approval. Monthly performance reporting must include maximum drawdown calculations, Sharpe ratios, and beta measurements against Russell 2000 Growth Index. Clients experiencing portfolio declines exceeding 20% from peak values require immediate suitability review and documentation of continued investment appropriateness.

Approval Authority: Senior Advisors (Tier 3+) must co-approve all aggressive equity allocations exceeding $250,000 in client accounts. New issue equity participation, IPO allocations, and private equity co-investments require Portfolio Committee pre-approval regardless of allocation size. This policy ensures adherence to firm risk management protocols while meeting our fiduciary obligations under applicable securities regulations.
